# Research - 2026-06-25

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins)

> 1-day delta on 2026-06-24. Three NEW items, all from 06-24/06-25
> commits. #1 (cline #11471) is a prefix-cache preservation pattern
> mapping to `core/agenticChat.ts:225` + `core/compaction.ts:155-180`
> where we EAGERLY mutate tool results -- breaking cache on every
> turn. #2 (cline #11803) is schema-aware tool-input coercion for
> `agenticChat.ts:225`'s `JSON.parse` -> Zod path that today rejects
> double-encoded array args. #3 (opencode #33226) is session-wide
> snapshot/revert layered on our existing `core/checkpoints.ts`
> per-file undo. opencode's 06-25 batch (33739, 33641, 33708, 33445)
> was internal Solid/Effect/HttpApi refactor with no surface for us.
> aider 30-day silent. claude-code 7/9 commits CHANGELOG.md.

## Executive Summary

- **cline #11471 -> Item #1 (~4h)**: We rewrite stale tool results
  in place via `compaction.ts:165`. Cline measured ~39% cache-miss
  vs ~2% with batched rewrites; SAP Orchestration + Anthropic uses
  `cache_control` so the 1.25x cache-write fee bites every turn.
- **cline #11803 -> Item #2 (~3h)**: `agenticChat.ts:225` parses
  `toolCall.function.arguments` once, hands to Zod. Double-encoded
  `{commands: "[\"a\",\"b\"]"}` is rejected -- wastes a turn.
  Schema-aware normalization is a one-pass fix.
- **opencode #33226 -> Item #3 (~6h)**: `checkpoints.ts` has
  per-file undo only. Add session-step snapshots reusing
  `FileCheckpoint.originalContent` bytes. No Git backend.
- **Carryover**: auto-implement queue EMPTY (25 PRs merged 7d).
  #772 demote-claude-code still pending after 5 briefs.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. Batch outdated-read rewrites to preserve prefix caches (Category: core, ~4h)

- **What**: Add `core/messageBuilder.ts` with a sticky
  `committedOutdatedRewrites` map keyed by `tool_call_id +
  filePath`. When a re-read of the same target appears later,
  queue the older result for rewrite to `[outdated - see latest
  content]` but only COMMIT the batch when reclaimable bytes
  exceed `ALEXI_MIN_OUTDATED_REWRITE_BYTES` (default 65536, ~16K
  tokens). Sticky commits so byte-identical transcripts replay
  and the cache prefix holds. Wire into
  `compaction.ts:pruneToolOutputs` and call from
  `streamingOrchestrator.ts` before the provider request. Env
  override + literal `disable` for rollback.
- **Why**: Eager mid-transcript mutation invalidates DeepSeek
  auto-cache, Anthropic `cache_control`, MiniMax prefix caching
  from the mutated message to tail. Every subsequent turn pays
  full uncached input plus the 1.25x cache-write multiplier.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11471 (commit `49884a1`, 2026-06-24,
  `+892 -16`,
  `sdk/packages/core/src/session/services/message-builder.ts`).
- **Category**: core. **Size**: small (~4h).

#### 2. Normalize JSON-like tool-input strings against schema (Category: tools, ~3h)

- **What**: Add
  `src/tool/normalizeJsonLikeStringsForSchema(input, jsonSchema)`
  walking the input against the tool's schema. For any field
  whose schema accepts `array` or `object` but whose value is a
  string starting with `[` or `{`, attempt `JSON.parse`; on
  success substitute the parsed value. Recurse for nested
  arrays/objects + `anyOf`/`oneOf` branches. Call from
  `core/agenticChat.ts:225` BEFORE `tool.execute(args)`. Test:
  schema `{commands: string[]}` + input
  `{commands: "[\"a\",\"b\"]"}` -> `{commands: ["a","b"]}`;
  schema `{text: string}` preserves JSON-looking strings verbatim.
- **Why**: SAP-routed mistralai variants and older
  OpenAI-compatible deployments double-encode array arguments.
  Today Zod rejects, `agenticChat.ts:228` returns "Invalid JSON
  in tool arguments", and the agent loop wastes a turn re-asking.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11803 (commit `96b29d7`, 2026-06-25,
  `+310 -4`, `sdk/packages/shared/src/parse/json.ts`,
  `sdk/packages/agents/src/agent-runtime.ts:1155`).
- **Category**: tools. **Size**: small (~3h).

#### 3. Session-wide snapshot + revert on top of file checkpoints (Category: command, ~6h)

- **What**: Add `core/snapshot.ts` capturing
  `{stepId, timestamp, sessionId, files: FileCheckpoint[],
  toolCalls: string[]}`. Persist under
  `~/.alexi/sessions/<id>/snapshots/<stepId>.json`. Register a
  snapshot per settled agent step via a
  `streamingOrchestrator.ts` settle hook. Add CLI
  `alexi revert --to <stepId> [--preview]`. Preview lists changed
  files since the snapshot; commit restores from each
  `FileCheckpoint.originalContent`. Files only -- do not touch
  messages.
- **Why**: Today undo is per-file. A 5-tool run touching 4 files
  needs 4 undo calls. Long-running agents routinely commit
  half-broken multi-file edits; session revert is the missing
  rollback unit. No new disk format -- reuses
  `FileCheckpoint.originalContent`. No Git backend (opencode
  added one, we don't need it).
- **Source**: sst/opencode#33226 (commit `9bb5370`, 2026-06-24,
  `+2311 -365`, `packages/core/src/snapshot.ts`,
  `packages/core/src/session/revert.ts`).
- **Category**: command. **Size**: medium (~6h).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Order**: #2 (3h) -> #1 (4h) -> #3 (6h). #2 + #1 both modify
   the `agenticChat.ts:225` neighborhood -- land #2 first so #1
   has a stable input contract.
2. **Cost lever**: #1 has the highest ROI -- prefix-cache breaks
   cost real money. Measure cache-miss rate in `core/stats.ts`
   before/after to validate.
3. **Pipeline health**: auto-implement queue EMPTY today, 25 PRs
   merged 7d. Engineering has bandwidth.
